items.title: Federal government prepares diphtheria support package as 220 cases reported

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

articles.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

gallery.altText: A man in a suit and tie looks sternly ahead.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a suit and tie looks sternly ahead.

items.title: Former NT child protection staff speak out after Kumanjayi Little Baby case

children.content: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

image.altText: Aboriginal woman, brown/gray hair tied back, blue pattern deress, serious expression, sitting, blurred greenery behind her

items.title: Man charged with Arnhem Land murder bailed to live with family 500km away

children.content: A 20-year-old man charged with the murder of another man during a brawl in Arnhem Land last month has been bailed to live with family far from the location of the death, in the Tiwi Islands.

image.altText: An aerial view of Milingimbi community in north-east Arnhem Land.

items.title: How a 'partial obscuration' phenomenon is impacting Darwin's sunsets

children.content: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.

gallery.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Beams of sunlight and shadows from clouds fill the pink and blue sky.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

thumbnail.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Beams of sunlight and shadows from clouds fill the pink and blue sky.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

items.title: NT records two deaths from rare mosquito-borne disease

children.content: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A female Aedes aegypti mosquito bites someone's skin.

items.title: Calls for hastily constructed NT evacuation site to be relocated

children.content: Residents in the regional NT town of Batchelor say they have been left in the dark about the future of a pop-up evacuation centre that was built earlier this year without their consultation for flood evacuees.

image.altText: A mattress hangs over a railing with generators in the background.

items.title: Teenager Bianca Adler becomes youngest Australian to summit Everest

children.content: A social media post just before 8am AEST announced that Bianca Adler had reached the top of the world's tallest mountain.

image.altText: Woman in hiking gear speaking in the dark.

gallery.altText: A girl wearing a helmet with stickets in a purple jacket holds a pick ice pick close to the camera, smiling widely.

thumbnail.altText: A girl wearing a helmet with stickets in a purple jacket holds a pick ice pick close to the camera, smiling widely.

items.title: Maldives investigates if Italian divers went too deep in fatal cave dive

children.content: A Maldives government official says a permit was given to study soft corals, but authorities were unaware the group planned to cave dive.

image.altText: divers

items.title: Israeli forces fire at Gaza flotilla, all boats intercepted, organisers say

children.content: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

image.altText: a war boat driving towards photographer

items.title: Fraud charge dropped against Olympic swimmer Neil Brooks

children.content: Neil Brooks and his wife Linda Brooks were both charged with one count of fraud in 2017, but were formally discharged on Wednesday.

image.altText: A group walking along a street.

items.title: Big guns return as Matildas call up teen goalkeeper

children.content: Mary Fowler, Ellie Carpenter and Steph Catley are back in the Matildas squad for games against Mexico, while a teenager has been called up.

image.altText: Mary Fowler runs with the ball

items.title: Federal government prepares diphtheria support package as 220 cases reported

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

items.title: 'Pure theft': Trump allies can seek payouts from $2.5b 'lawfare' fund

children.content: The US has set up a fund to compensate Americans deemed victims of "lawfare". Democrats say it is "pure theft of public funds" to pay the president's allies.

image.altText: Donald Trump sits behind a laptop in a courtroom. He is frowning.

items.title: Life on one of the world's most dangerous waterways

children.content: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.

gallery.altText: three women in the shallows of a beach swimming and taking photos.

thumbnail.altText: three women in the shallows of a beach swimming and taking photos.

items.title: What life is like as an older parent, or being born to one

children.content: After publishing our story on having babies in your 40s, we heard from readers on what being, or having, an older parent is like.

image.altText: couple with pram on sidewalk

items.title: 'Absolute madness': How a costume rock duo are breaking the internet

children.content: French Canadian duo Angine de Poitrine are riding a wave of interest after a video of their live performance exploded in popularity, aided by a comical reaction by many on YouTube struggling to understand how their music is made.

image.altText: Two people in spotted costumes.

items.title: The men offering an alternative to 'manosphere' influencers

children.content: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?

gallery.altText: A selfie of Caleb Ferdinand and his toddler, taken inside a car.
A man in his late 20s with dark brown hair, smiling, sitting in farming equipment

A man in his late 20s with dark brown hair, smiling, sitting in farming equipment

items.title: Infant death sparks call for use of church land to tackle homelessness

children.content: Church land is being touted as a potential solution to the homelessness crisis in a southern NSW city still reeling from the death of a newborn baby in a tent almost three weeks ago.

image.altText: Homeless setup near Wagga beach with backdrop of church

items.title: 'I'm over it': Minister lashes City of Perth over 'diabolical' culture

children.content: The "diabolical" City of Perth should be merged with its neighbouring council, a WA government minister has claimed, describing the "pettiness, infighting, immaturity" by elected members as "galling."

image.altText: John Carey sits with a serious contemplative expression in a TV studio. A bright beam of light shines across his face.

items.title: ASX falls as inflation worries lead to US government debt sell-off

children.content: The Australian share market fell after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it. Follow live.

image.altText: Shares on a screen at the ASX

items.title: One killed, four injured in 'brazen, violent attack' inside Sydney home

children.content: A man with links to rap group OneFour has died and four others have been seriously injured following a Canley Heights shooting, which is believed to be organised crime-related.

image.altText: Police officers in suburban street and inside a brick home

items.title: Miners plan to retrofit WA nickel plants to cash in on high gold prices

children.content: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

gallery.altText: A mining executive in high-vis standing in front of a mill.

thumbnail.altText: A mining executive in high-vis standing in front of a mill.

items.title: More ISIS-linked women and children set to return to Australia

children.content: Six Australian women, along with their children and grandchildren, look likely to leave a camp in Syria within days.

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

items.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

image.altText: A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt

items.title: Arsenal ends 22-year Premier League drought after Man City draw

children.content: Arsenal's long wait for a Premier League title comes to an end as title challengers Manchester City drop points at Bournemouth.

image.altText: Mikel Arteta and Leandro Trossard celebrate together while the Arsenal squad convene behind them

items.title: Luai and Tigers headline NRL's Las Vegas quartet for 2027 season-openers

children.content: Jarome Luai will begin his final season with the Wests Tigers in Las Vegas, with the NRL unveiling its teams for next year's season-openers.

image.altText: Jarome Luai lookes dejected after a Wests Tigers' loss during the 2025 NRL season.

items.title: Landscape in outback SA 'permanently changed' by recent earthquake

children.content: Cracks in the ground in outback Australia caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake could help researchers predict future seismic activity.

gallery.altText: Dry, grassy ground with a crack going along the ground through the dirt, with a person in the background
brown dirt with a long crack going along the ground

brown dirt with a long crack going along the ground

items.title: Son found guilty of helping drug-runner dad with Newcastle cocaine shipment

children.content: A Queensland man has been found guilty of involvement with his father's attempt to smuggle a massive amount of cocaine into the NSW port city.

image.altText: A young man with dark wavy hair posing outdoors in soft light.

items.title: Why is there no vaccine for this Ebola outbreak?

children.content: The latest Ebola outbreak is caused by a strain of virus discovered nearly two decades ago, yet there is no vaccine. So what does this mean for people affected? And can vaccines be fast-tracked?

image.altText: Thermometers on a table with a healthcare worker in the background.

items.title: Complaint from ex-boss led to investigation into embattled mental health clinic

children.content: Tasmania's largest private mental health hospital, which was given a $2 million lifeline by the state government last year, had its corporate governance placed under review, following a formal complaint by the facility's former chief executive.

image.altText: Generic photos of a mental health facility.

items.title: Fears mount for victims of domestic violence ahead of WA court closures

children.content: The doors of 26 regional and remote courthouses in WA will shut for three months, leaving lawyers confused and concerned about consequences for domestic violence legal matters.

image.altText: The front of a court house

items.title: Luxury apartments greenlit under affordable housing scheme

children.content: Property developers are using an affordable housing scheme to bypass normal planning requirements and win fast-tracked approval for contentious projects across Melbourne.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a multi-story building development, alongside a busy road.

items.title: Homesharer allegedly used disabled man's bank account to spend $54,000

children.content: A 70-year-old appointed homesharer is charged with 264 fraud offences after allegedly spending money belonging to an intellectually disabled man he was engaged to support.

image.altText: A cllose-up shot of a man with short grey hair and a moustache, wearing spectacles and a dark polo shirt, outside court

items.title: Researchers uncover longest whale journey ever documented

children.content: Scientists analysing a database of photos find two humpback whales travelled from Queensland to Brazil, covering a record distance of 14,200km.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of three humpback whales in the ocean

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of three humpback whales in the ocean
